name: Arabi Sugar Workers
description_short: Arabi Sugar Workers Federal Credit Union is a small not-for-profit credit union established in 1970 serving manufacturing sector employees in New Orleans with savings accounts, auto loans, and personal banking.
description_long:
Arabi Sugar Workers Federal Credit Union was established in 1970 and is headquartered in New Orleans, Louisiana. As a federally chartered credit union, it operates as a member-owned, not-for-profit financial cooperative. The institution maintains a single branch location at 11448 S Easterlyn Circle and stewards approximately $1.8 million in assets while serving over 300 loyal members with a small dedicated staff of over 2 employees.

The credit union offers a range of financial products tailored to manufacturing sector employees, including regular savings accounts, share draft checking accounts, auto loans (both new and used vehicles), personal loans, and mortgage options. They provide competitive interest rates—including 5% on new vehicle loans and 7% on used vehicle loans—along with online and mobile banking capabilities, bill pay services, and financial calculators. Members can access financial guidance through their community Q&A forum and benefit from the institution's not-for-profit status, which typically translates to better rates and lower fees compared to for-profit banks.

What distinguishes Arabi Sugar Workers FCU is its specialization in serving the manufacturing sector with industry-specific financial solutions and its deep community roots in New Orleans dating back over 50 years. As a small, member-focused institution, it prioritizes personalized service rather than trying to compete on scale. The credit union emphasizes member relationships and offers accessible customer service through phone contact at (504) 522-2525 and online banking options.

However, prospective members should note several limitations: the institution is very small with only 1 branch and 0 ATMs, creating accessibility challenges for members outside the immediate New Orleans area. The website indicates service issues may be occurring, and the lobby is currently closed. With only $1.8 million in assets and 300+ members, this is a micro-credit union with limited resources compared to larger financial institutions. Membership eligibility is restricted to those in manufacturing sectors served by the credit union, and there are currently no member reviews available online to provide insight into member experiences. Many banks offer installment loans with fixed monthly payments, giving borrowers a clear payoff timeline.
pros: Not-for-profit credit union structure offers better rates and lower fees than for-profit banks
Competitive auto loan rates: 5% for new vehicles and 7% for used vehicles
Online and mobile banking available for both iOS and Android devices
Offers multiple loan types including auto loans, personal loans, and mortgages
Small, community-focused institution with personalized service approach
Over 50 years of history serving New Orleans manufacturing community since 1970
Free financial tools including mortgage calculator and online bill pay for members
cons: Only 1 physical branch with 0 ATMs limits accessibility for members outside immediate New Orleans area
Very small institution ($1.8M in assets) with limited resources and only 2+ staff members
Website indicates the credit union may be experiencing service issues and lobby is currently closed
Membership eligibility restricted to specific manufacturing sectors, not open to general public
No member reviews available online to evaluate member satisfaction and service quality
best_for: Manufacturing sector employees in New Orleans seeking a community-focused credit union
Local residents wanting to support a small, not-for-profit financial institution
Members seeking competitive auto loan rates with personalized service
diagnosis: Arabi Sugar Workers FCU is best suited for manufacturing sector employees and residents in the New Orleans area seeking competitive rates and personalized service from a small, not-for-profit credit union. The primary caveat is that this is a micro-credit union with just one branch and no ATMs, making it impractical for members needing broader geographic access or those outside manufacturing sectors, and current service issues warrant verification before joining.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
